chapter: Chapter 3: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: State the condition for a pair of linear equations to have a unique solution.
questions.answer: When the lines intersect at one point.

questions.question: State the condition for a pair of linear equations to have no solution.
questions.answer: When the lines are parallel.

questions.question: State the condition for a pair of linear equations to have infinitely many solutions.
questions.answer: When the lines are coincident.

questions.question: What does the condition a₁/a₂ ≠ b₁/b₂ indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates intersecting lines.

questions.question: What does the condition a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂ indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates parallel lines.

questions.question: What does the condition a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂ indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates coincident lines.

questions.question: Why does graphical method give only approximate solutions?
questions.answer: Because accuracy depends on scale and drawing.

questions.question: Which method is preferred for exact solutions and why?
questions.answer: Algebraic methods, because they give exact values.

questions.question: When is elimination method preferred?
questions.answer: When coefficients of a variable are equal or easily made equal.

questions.question: When is substitution method preferred?
questions.answer: When one variable has coefficient 1.

questions.question: What is the standard form required for cross-multiplication method?
questions.answer: ax + by + c = 0.

questions.question: What is meant by an independent system?
questions.answer: A system having a unique solution.

questions.question: What is meant by a dependent system?
questions.answer: A system having infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: What is meant by a simultaneous equation?
questions.answer: Equations solved together at the same time.

questions.question: How many solutions can a pair of linear equations never have?
questions.answer: Exactly two solutions.

questions.question: What does the intersection point satisfy?
questions.answer: Both equations simultaneously.

questions.question: Why do parallel lines not intersect?
questions.answer: Because they have the same slope.

questions.question: Why do coincident lines have infinite solutions?
questions.answer: Because every point on one line lies on the other.

questions.question: What is the graphical meaning of inconsistent system?
questions.answer: The lines are parallel.

questions.question: What is the graphical meaning of consistent system?
questions.answer: The lines intersect or coincide.

questions.question: Why are two equations required to find two unknowns?
questions.answer: Because one equation is insufficient to determine both values.

questions.question: What is the advantage of elimination method?
questions.answer: It simplifies equations by removing one variable.

questions.question: What is the advantage of substitution method?
questions.answer: It reduces the number of variables.

questions.question: What does slope represent?
questions.answer: It represents the rate of change of y with respect to x.

questions.question: Linear equations are used to model what type of relationships?
questions.answer: Relationships with constant rate of change.
